---
title: "Figure 1"
subtitle: "Creating a map to show the geographic distribution of studies included in the meta-analysis and the number of effect sizes in each study."
author: "Dillan Hoyt"
execute: 
  eval: true
  echo: false
  message: false
editor: visual
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
toc-title: Contents
---

```{r}
#Adding packages: 
libs <- c(
  "sf", "rnaturalearth", "rnaturalearthdata", "ggplot2", "ggrepel",
  "dplyr", "countrycode", "scales"
)   
invisible(lapply(libs, library, character.only = TRUE))

#adding information of each site and 
meta_bats <- read.csv("Metadata_Figure1.csv")

# load world
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# canonicalise meta table
meta_bats <- meta_bats %>%
  mutate(
    Country_raw = as.character(Country),
    Country_std = countrycode(Country_raw, "country.name", "country.name", warn = FALSE)
  ) %>%
  # fallback manual recodes for common edge cases you already know
  mutate(
    Country_std = case_when(
      Country_raw %in% c("U.S.A", "USA", "Usa", "United States Of America") ~ "United States",
      Country_raw %in% c("French Guiana") ~ "France",
      Country_raw %in% c("Borneo","Malaysia Borneo","Malysia Borneo","Borneo ") ~ "Malaysia",
      TRUE ~ Country_std
    )
  )


# build paper-level unique points (one point per Paper_ID)
paper_points <- meta_bats %>%
  distinct(Paper_ID, Country, Longitude, Latitude, n_total, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Longitude) & !is.na(Latitude)) %>%
  # optional: if there are multiple rows per paper with different coords take the first
  group_by(Paper_ID) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  ungroup()

# convert to sf points (WGS84)
paper_sf <- st_as_sf(paper_points, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)

# world polygons
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# spatially match each paper point to the world polygon it falls in
paper_match <- st_join(paper_sf, world %>% select(name), left = TRUE, join = st_within) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  rename(matched_country = name)

# count unique papers per matched country 
country_counts <- paper_match %>%
  distinct(Paper_ID, matched_country) %>%
  filter(!is.na(matched_country)) %>%
  count(matched_country, name = "n_papers")

# left-join counts onto world using the polygon name
world_meta <- world %>%
  left_join(country_counts, by = c("name" = "matched_country"))


#French Guiana is currently appearing as France, so we need to derive a polygon for FG and assign it the correct count (2 papers).
fg_bbox <- st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = -55.0, xmax = -50.0, ymin = 2.0, ymax = 6.0), crs = st_crs(4326)))

# get France polygon from the Natural Earth `world` sf
fr_row <- world %>% filter(name == "France")
if (nrow(fr_row) == 0) stop("No France polygon found in 'world' object.")

# intersect France with bbox -> French Guiana polygon
fg_poly <- st_intersection(fr_row, fg_bbox)
if (nrow(fg_poly) == 0) {
  # try slightly larger bbox if first intersection failed
  fg_bbox2 <- st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = -56, xmax = -49, ymin = 1.5, ymax = 7.0), crs = st_crs(4326)))
  fg_poly <- st_intersection(fr_row, fg_bbox2)
}

# assign name + count (2 studies as you requested)
fg_poly <- fg_poly %>% mutate(name = "French Guiana", n_papers = 2L)

# prepare mainland France as unfilled (NA) to avoid colouring metropolitan France
france_mainland <- fr_row %>% mutate(n_papers = NA_integer_)

# build updated world_meta:
# remove original France row from world_meta if present, then bind mainland France (NA) + FG polygon
world_meta_no_fr <- world_meta %>% filter(name != "France")

# ensure fg_poly has same columns as world_meta_no_fr (keep geometry + name + n_papers)
fg_poly_to_bind <- fg_poly %>% select(all_of(intersect(names(world_meta_no_fr), names(fg_poly))))

world_meta_updated <- bind_rows(
  world_meta_no_fr,
  # include mainland France geometry but with NA fill
  france_mainland %>% select(names(world_meta_no_fr)),
  # add French Guiana polygon (with n_papers = 2)
  fg_poly_to_bind %>% mutate(!!!setNames(rep(list(NA), length(setdiff(names(world_meta_no_fr), names(fg_poly_to_bind)))), setdiff(names(world_meta_no_fr), names(fg_poly_to_bind))))
)

# plotting palette 
cb_ramp <- c("#440154FF", "#365C8DFF", "#1FA187FF", "#9FDA3AFF", "#FDE725FF")

max_val <- max(world_meta_updated$n_papers, na.rm = TRUE)

# compute b_cnt
b_cnt <- country_counts %>% filter(matched_country %in% c("Brazil","Brasil")) %>% summarise(n = sum(n_papers, na.rm=TRUE)) %>% pull(n)
b_cnt <- ifelse(length(b_cnt)==0 || is.na(b_cnt), 0, b_cnt)

# set Brazil n_papers in world_meta_updated
world_meta_updated <- world_meta_updated %>%
  mutate(n_papers = ifelse(name == "Brazil", as.integer(b_cnt), n_papers))

# label centroids for countries with studies
label_df <- world_meta_updated %>%
  filter(!is.na(n_papers) & n_papers > 0) %>%
  mutate(centroid = st_point_on_surface(geometry)) %>%
  mutate(X = st_coordinates(centroid)[, 1],
         Y = st_coordinates(centroid)[, 2]) %>%
  st_drop_geometry()


  
# final plot: country fill + paper points sized by n_total 
fig_map_meta <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world_meta_updated, aes(fill = n_papers), colour = "white", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_sf(
    data = paper_sf,
    aes(size = n_total),
    shape = 21, fill = "black", alpha = 0.7, color = "white", stroke = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_label_repel(
    data = label_df,
    aes(x = X, y = Y, label = name),
    size = 3,
    box.padding = 0.4,
    point.padding = 0.6,
    segment.color = "grey40",
    segment.size = 0,
    fill = "white",
    color = "black",
    max.overlaps = 20,
    force = 4
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = cb_ramp,
    limits = c(0, max_val),
    oob = scales::squish,
    trans = "sqrt",
    na.value = "grey92",
    name = "Countries (no. of studies)",
    guide = guide_colorbar(
      barwidth = unit(12, "cm"),
      barheight = unit(0.6, "cm")
    )
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    range = c(2, 8),
    breaks = c(100, 1000, 2000, 3000),
    labels = scales::comma,
    name = "No. effect sizes per study"
  ) +
  coord_sf(expand = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Global distribution of studies and sampling points",
    subtitle = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey90"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )

# show plot
print(fig_map_meta)

ggsave("global_study_map_points.pdf", plot = fig_map_meta, width = 11, height = 7, units = "in", dpi = 300)



```
